mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings expectations and pushing S&P 500 forward returns positive over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with rising industrial production signal synchronized demand and supply recovery, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of historical episodes, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, rising real activity forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic momentum, supporting corporate revenue growth and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month directional accuracy of PAYEMS growth falls below 50%, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between positive and negative payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit outstanding declines for two consecutive months, it signals tightening household balance sheets, reducing aggregate demand and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 65% of instances following two consecutive months of RSAFS decline, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production paired with a rising federal funds rate indicates supply-led growth that outpaces monetary tightening, easing recession fears and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of historical episodes, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment, rising housing starts act as a leading indicator of economic stabilization, triggering a relief rally and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly positive when HOUST rises while UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.
